content.problemStatement:
Write a C++ program to:



Create a class Demo




Include:



A constructor that displays the message: "Object Created"
A destructor that displays the message: "Object Deleted"



Create an object in main() and observe the output.
content.outputFormat: Two lines printed to standard output:\n1. "Object Created"\n2. "Object Deleted"

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program creates an object (or simulates it) whose constructor prints the first line. When the program ends, the destructor prints the second line.

Even if the program were extended to create multiple objects, each object's constructor and destructor would produce the same two messages in order; with a single object the output appears exactly once each.
The messages are independent of any input; they always appear exactly once for the single Demo instance.

solution.approach: The task is to demonstrate the use of a constructor and a destructor. Create a class Demo with a constructor that prints "Object Created" and a destructor that prints "Object Deleted". Instantiate a Demo object in main; the constructor runs at creation and the destructor runs automatically when the object goes out of scope at program termination.
